exemplar.title: To what extent does the hippocampus explain episodic memory decline in late-onset Alzheimerâs Disease patients?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does the hippocampus explain episodic memory decline in late-onset Alzheimerâs Disease patients?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with clear and appropriate purpose and focus
Research question is clearly stated, focused and consistently referenced throughout the investigation
weaknesses: Methodology lacks detailed search strategy (databases, keywords, inclusion/exclusion criteria) reducing transparency and reproducibility
Minor tangential sentences in the introduction detract slightly from the otherwise precise focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (topic communication and research question) clearly meet the highest band descriptors, while the methodology is âmostly completeâ but missing key specifics. This justifies a mid-band score of 5 out of 6.

strengths: Excellent integration of over thirty peer-reviewed sources with coherent application to the research question
Accurate and consistent use of specialist terminology and concepts (e.g., voxel-based morphometry, neurogenesis)
weaknesses: Occasional imprecise clinical terminology (describing LOAD as âmalignantâ)
Minor conflation of short-term and long-term memory constructs and reliance on a non-peer-reviewed site for WMM explanation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates sophisticated subject knowledge and consistent, accurate use of concepts, placing it at the upper end of the 5-6 band. A few inaccuracies prevent a full 6, so 5 out of 6 is appropriate.

strengths: Extensive, relevant research consistently tied to the research question
Balanced evaluation with methodological critique and consideration of counter-evidence
Nuanced, well-structured conclusion acknowledging multifactorial influences
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally lacks statistical metrics (effect sizes, p-values) for deeper quantitative evaluation
Evaluative synthesis does not propose specific future research directions
Some critical points remain generic rather than deeply contextualized
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality aligns with the top band, but limitations in depth of analysis and evaluation place the overall performance at the lower end of the 7-9 band. A score of 7 out of 12 reflects strong critical thinking tempered by these weaknesses.

strengths: Formatting and layout meet IB EE conventions with professional consistency
Complete structure including title page, table of contents, chapter headings, conclusion and bibliography
weaknesses: Minor typographical errors and occasional inconsistent use of italics
Lower-level headings lack page numbers, slightly hindering quick navigation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation fully satisfies the top-band descriptors, with only minor lapses that do not compromise readability or professionalism. The maximum mark of 4 is therefore justified.

strandSelections.reasoning: Specialist terminologyâhippocampal sub-regions, voxel-based morphometry, glucocorticoids, episodic bufferâis used frequently and usually accurately, demonstrating command of psychological and neuroscientific vocabulary.  Concepts such as neurogenesis, neuroinflammation and the Papez circuit are explained clearly for the intended audience.  Minor instances of imprecise wording (e.g., âmalignantâ LOAD, occasional conflation of STM/LTM processes) occur but do not seriously impede understanding.  The consistent, largely accurate deployment of discipline-specific language justifies a high score within the top band.

Formatting is largely professional: uniform fonts, margin consistency, correctly styled figure captions, and accurate citation matching the bibliography.  Required elementsâtitle, word count, page numbersâare present.  A handful of typographical slips (e.g., double punctuation, inconsistent use of italics) and slightly compressed figure images prevent a perfect presentation, but layout standards are overwhelmingly met, justifying full marks in this band.

The essay integrates more than thirty peer-reviewed sources spanning neuroanatomy, imaging, and cognitive theory.  Summaries of studies (e.g., Jack et al., Park et al., Joubert et al.) are technically sound, and the student applies findings to the research question rather than merely describing them.  Occasional slipsâsuch as labelling LOAD âmalignantâ or conflating working memory with long-term processesâslightly dent clinical precision, yet overall knowledge is sophisticated and coherently applied.  These strengths align with the upper end of the 5-6 band, though the few inaccuracies prevent an unreserved 6.

A literature-review strategy is identified and an appropriate mix of experimental papers, neuro-imaging studies, and meta-analyses is consulted.  Nevertheless, the methodology section is largely declarative: it names broad source types but omits explicit search engines, key terms, time frames, or inclusion/exclusion criteria.  No justification is given for why particular studies (e.g., Joubert vs. others) were favoured, nor is there evidence of systematic source evaluation beyond passing comments on validity.  Hence the approach is âmostly completeâ with some informed selection evident, but it falls short of the transparency and rigour required for the 5-6 band.

The student opens with a coherent, engaging overview that clearly defines the field of cognition, narrows rapidly to memory, and finally specifies episodic memory decline in late-onset Alzheimerâs disease (LOAD). Key biological structures (e.g., hippocampus, dentate gyrus) and key terms (ApoE4, amyloid-Î²) are introduced accurately and linked to the stated purpose. Each chapter title and sub-heading is explicitly anchored to the focus on âTo what extent does the hippocampus explain episodic memory decline â¦,â so the reader can follow the line of inquiry without ambiguity. Minor tangential sentences on the wonder of cognition do not materially undermine clarity. Overall, communication of topic, purpose and focus is consistently precise and effective, fully meeting the top-band descriptor.

Research coverage is extensive: longitudinal studies, controlled experiments, neuro-imaging projects and meta-analyses are marshalled to address both hippocampal and non-hippocampal explanations.  Source selection is consistently relevant to the RQ; there is no evident padding with tangential literature.  Each citation directly supports a line of argument (e.g., using Park et al. for connectivity, Schmaal et al. for depression link).  While search procedures are not fully documented (a methodology limitation), the final bibliography and in-text integration reflect an excellent breadth and pertinence of research, meriting the 10-12 band.

The research question is succinctly phrased, conceptually tight (linking one brain structure to one clearly defined memory system within a specific patient group), and it drives every subsequent section.  The question is restated in the introduction, echoed in chapter headings (CLOA / BLOA), and revisited in the conclusion, demonstrating sustained focus.  Evaluative comments throughout (e.g., on hippocampal atrophy vs. extra-hippocampal factors) show the student keeping the discussion tethered to the RQ.  The precision of the operationalisation (hippocampus â episodic memory decline in LOAD) ensures the work remains squarely within the scope of an EE, thereby satisfying the highest band.

The report adheres to IB EE conventions: title page, abstract substitute, table of contents, logical chaptering (CLOA, BLOA), conclusion, appendices, and a complete bibliography.  Signposting is clear and pagination consistent, enabling easy navigation.  Minor heading mis-numberings and an isolated awkward page break persist, but they do not impede overall readability.  Thus the structure is âclearly appropriate,â earning the top band for this strand.

The discussion culminates in a nuanced conclusion that the hippocampus is âsignificant but not sufficient,â acknowledging multifactorial influences.  Strengths and limitations of individual studies are identified (sample size, ecological validity, imaging artefacts), and the student reflects on how these affect certainty.  Some evaluative points, however, remain generic (e.g., repeated notes on âlow ecological validityâ for any lab study) and the synthesis stops short of proposing clear future research directions.  The argument is reasoned and partially critical, corresponding to the lower end of the 7-9 band.

The student moves beyond summary, dissecting methodologies (e.g., reliability of neuropsychological batteries, fMRI temporal resolution) and linking empirical results to theoretical models (WMM, neurotrophic hypothesis).  Counter-evidence (depression, ozone exposure) is weighed to moderate causal claims, showing balanced reasoning.  Nonetheless, analytical depth is occasionally surface-level: statistical metrics (effect sizes, p-values) are rarely reported; some causal language persists when only correlational data are available.  These limitations position the work solidly within the 7-9 band, with room for richer quantitative scrutiny to reach 10-12.

annotations.comment: Acknowledgement of neglected holistic factors in studies highlights the studentâs capacity for critical breadth in evaluation.
Table of contents is clearly structured with uniform formatting and accurate chapter listings, enhancing navigability.
Ozone study summary omits discussion of environmental and cultural confounds; incorporate these factors to strengthen contextual analysis.
Conclusion synthesizes findings well but fails to propose specific future research directions; include clear recommendations for further study.
Word count is provided and stays within the 4000-word limit, demonstrating adherence to formatting requirements.
Introduction opening is overly broad and includes tangential material; focus more directly on research context to improve coherence.
Reliance on a non-peer-reviewed DP Psychology site for the WMM description weakens source credibility; prioritize primary academic literature.
Identification of the WMMâs limitation regarding LTM and component interactions demonstrates clear theoretical evaluation.
Joubert et al. summary omits statistical details (effect sizes, p-values); integrating quantitative metrics would strengthen analytical depth.
Evaluation of ecological validity and participant matching in the Joubert study reflects balanced and insightful methodological critique.
Castel et al. methodology lacks rationale for participant selection and value-directed word categorization; clarify to improve methodological clarity.
Jack et al. (1999) is dated and does not address advances in imaging; consider discussing modern methodologies to maintain relevance.
Title is clearly stated at the start of the report, effectively communicating the investigation topic and meeting IB requirements.
The research question is succinctly phrased, focused, and consistently referenced throughout, effectively guiding the investigation.
Lower-level headings lack corresponding page numbers, which may hinder quick navigation to specific subsections.
Page numbers are present and consistently formatted in the footer, supporting ease of reference.
Methodology lacks specifics on search strategy (databases, keywords, inclusion/exclusion criteria), reducing transparency and reproducibility.
Discussion of construct validity, control group use, and mundane realism in the Castel study demonstrates nuanced critical evaluation.
The fMRI study by Park et al. uses a relatively small sample, which may limit statistical power and generalizability; reflect on this impact.
Describing LOAD as âmalignantâ is imprecise in a clinical context; prefer accurate terminology to maintain academic rigor.
Potential confirmation bias in the meta-analysis is not discussed; recommend outlining strategies to mitigate bias in source synthesis.
Introduction entry in contents uses generic labeling; consider adding sub-section titles for greater specificity.
Chapter headings are clearly labeled and hierarchically formatted, supporting logical structure and ease of navigation.
Sub-heading phrasing is overly verbose; shortening titles would improve readability and audience engagement.
Correlation between depression and memory decline is presented without addressing bidirectional ambiguity; specify limitations for causal inference.
